steps.title: Identify the linear relationship
steps.content: The graph shows a straight line passing through the origin, indicating volume is directly proportional to temperature: y = kx where k is the constant of proportionality.
From the graph, at a temperature of 250 kelvins the volume reads 3.5 liters. Thus the slope is $$k = \frac{3.5}{250} = \frac{7}{500}$$ liters per kelvin.
steps.title: Find the slope from a convenient point

steps.content: For a temperature of 500 kelvins, the volume is $$y = k \times 500 = \frac{7}{500} \times 500 = 7$$ liters. That matches choice C — C is correct.
steps.title: Calculate the volume at 500 K and select answer
